description: This line chart presents the relationship between solidification fraction $z_s$ (x-axis, ranging 0.80 to 1.00) and logarithmic values of TiN precipitation equilibrium constant $\log K_{\text{TiN}}$ (solid lines) and actual activity product $\log Q_{\text{TiN}}$ (dashed lines, y-axis, ranging -4.5 to -2.5) for three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel samples: 1# with ~1 wt% Al (black lines), 2# with ~3 wt% Al (blue lines), 3# with ~5 wt% Al (red lines). The intersection point of each sample's $\log K_{\text{TiN}}$ and $\log Q_{\text{TiN}}$ curves marks the solidification fraction at which TiN inclusions initiate precipitation, measured as 0.89 for 1#, 0.93 for 2#, and 0.94 for 3#. The observed trend demonstrates that increasing Al content delays TiN precipitation, as precipitation occurs at a higher, later-stage solidification fraction with rising Al addition. This figure supports the core conclusion that elevated Al content modifies the solidification route of the tested low-density steels, leading to delayed TiN inclusion precipitation.
